plan.visual_reading: The arc opens on one forsaken man on a lone cross in an immense emptiness, then the psalm's last lines reach outward and the cross on its hill is shown against a whole world horizon. The hero is the cross radiating light to the far edges of the land — the reach the song foresaw. From the tiny hill in one corner of the empire, the gospel goes out by road and sea; the ancient promise to Abraham (all nations blessed) and the light to the Gentiles carry the OT echoes; kindreds of many lands gather in worship and pilgrims turn nation after nation. It lands on a single small figure on a far road turning toward the light, an open way left for whoever will come, and the cross cresting the whole earth at dawn.
plan.red_team_notes: Jaded Viewer risks: (1) 'the nations' can become a repetitive parade of bowing crowds — I varied it across gathered worshippers (8), a pilgrim procession (11), a distant people first catching light (9), and a single lone turner (12), each a distinct framing and idea. (2) 'light to the nations' invites the banned heaven-opens beam — I used a low-horizon dawn reaching across dark ground, never a vertical god-beam. (3) The global theme could float free of the cross — every wide scene keeps the cross or its light as the source. (4) Avoided modern globe/map anachronisms; the 'world' is rendered as far lands, seas and horizons.

scenes.title: One Man, Alone
scenes.purpose: open on the lone forsaken man dying alone — the contrast the hook turns on
scenes.rationale: isolation rather than a crowd; the worldwide theme begins from one man
scenes.visible_elements: a lone cross with one slumped robed figure, a vast empty darkening sky, a bare hill falling away on every side
scenes.subject_block: a single crucified figure on a lone cross against a vast empty darkening sky, the robed body slumped in isolation, no crowd and no other figure near, the bare hill falling away into shadow on every side, the lone cross small and stark at the centre of an immense emptiness
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of one man utterly alone
macro_elements: the upright beam against the void

scenes.title: The Song Opens Its Arms
scenes.purpose: the psalm's closing lines reaching outward to the nations
scenes.rationale: the scroll's light widening outward carries the turn without text overlay
scenes.visible_elements: an ancient Hebrew scroll, lower lines caught in a widening warm light, upper lines in shadow
scenes.subject_block: an ancient Hebrew scroll spread open across pale stone, its lower lines of script caught in a widening warm light while the upper lines rest in shadow, the parchment worn and fibrous, the ink old and cracked, a single rolled wooden stave at the side, the warmth opening outward toward the lit lower edge of the page
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of a song reaching outward
macro_elements: the lit lower lines of script

scenes.title: All the Ends of the World
scenes.purpose: hold the cross against a whole-world horizon
scenes.rationale: the psalm's global reach as one coherent composition
scenes.visible_elements: the cross on a hill, a vast horizon, distant lands, dim multitudes turning
scenes.subject_block: the cross on a high hill at the centre, the robed crucified figure calm in completion, and beyond it a vast world horizon opening on every side, subtle background vignettes fading into shadow suggesting distant far cities, a wide sea, far mountain ranges, and dim multitudes of peoples turning toward the hill, the central cross dominant and the far lands half-dissolved in haze
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of one cross reaching a whole world

scenes.title: The Reach of the Cross
scenes.purpose: the gospel pivot — the cross radiating light to the far edges of the land
scenes.rationale: the CTA's destination; iconic, near-still, reverent, bookends the cut
scenes.visible_elements: the lifted cross, a luminous dawn sky, warm light spreading to far horizons
scenes.emotional_tone: the cross reaching the ends of the earth
scenes.subject_block: the cross lifted high seen from a low angle, the robed crucified figure against a luminous dawn sky, warm light breaking from behind the upright beam and spreading outward across far horizons on either side, the bare hill at the base, the whole composition radiating from the single lifted cross toward the distant edges of the land
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of the cross reaching the ends of the earth
macro_elements: the far horizons on either side

scenes.title: One Corner of the Empire
scenes.purpose: the smallness of the place against the vastness of the world
scenes.rationale: shows 'one corner of the Roman Empire' literally and modestly
scenes.visible_elements: a small bare hill with distant crosses, dwarfed by a vast Roman-era landscape
scenes.emotional_tone: a small place beneath a vast world
scenes.subject_block: a small bare hill bearing three distant crosses, dwarfed beneath an immense Roman-era landscape of far plains, a remote walled city and a distant aqueduct, the sky vast and pale above, the hill of execution tiny in one corner of an enormous world
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of a small place beneath a vast world

scenes.title: The Gospel Goes Out
scenes.purpose: the gospel's going-out to the nations after the cross and empty tomb
scenes.rationale: the historical spread shown, not a doctrinal claim overlaid
scenes.visible_elements: travellers on a road carrying a scroll, a ship with a sail on a far sea
scenes.emotional_tone: good news carried to far places
scenes.subject_block: robed figures set out along a worn road that winds toward a distant horizon, one carrying a scroll, a small ship with a single sail upon a far sea beyond, subtle background vignettes fading into shadow suggesting a distant city gate, a mountain pass, a lamp passed from hand to hand, and a far shore, the travelling figures dominant in the foreground and the far places half-dissolved in haze
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of good news carried to far places
macro_elements: the small sail on the far sea
vignettes: a lamp passed hand to hand

scenes.title: In Thee All Nations Blessed
scenes.purpose: the ancient promise that all nations would be blessed (Gen 22:18 -> Gal 3:8)
scenes.rationale: a real NT-anchored OT echo of the nations blessed in Abraham's seed
scenes.visible_elements: an aged patriarch beneath countless stars, distant peoples, a child
scenes.emotional_tone: an ancient promise of all nations
scenes.subject_block: an aged patriarch standing beneath a vast field of countless stars, his face lifted and one hand open toward the night sky, subtle background vignettes fading into shadow suggesting numberless stars, distant tents of a far people, a child at his side, and remote shores, the standing patriarch dominant and the promise of nations half-dissolved across the heavens
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of an ancient promise of all nations
macro_elements: the open hand toward the sky
vignettes: distant tents of a far people

scenes.title: Kindreds of the Nations
scenes.purpose: all the kindreds of the nations worshipping (Ps 22:27 fulfilled)
scenes.rationale: varied peoples turned up in worship, their homelands behind them
scenes.visible_elements: a company of peoples of many nations, faces lifted in worship, far homelands
scenes.emotional_tone: all the kindreds of the earth worshipping
scenes.subject_block: a close-gathered company of peoples of many nations, varied in dress and feature, their faces lifted in worship toward a warm light at the edge of the scene, robes of many lands among them, subtle background vignettes fading into shadow suggesting a far desert caravan, an island shore, a northern forest, and a distant city, the gathered worshippers dominant and their far homelands half-dissolved behind them
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of all the kindreds of the earth worshipping
macro_elements: the warm light at the edge

scenes.title: A Light to the Gentiles
scenes.purpose: light reaching a people in darkness (Isaiah 49:6 -> Acts 13:47)
scenes.rationale: low-horizon dawn reaching across dark ground, never a vertical god-beam
scenes.visible_elements: a distant people at the edge of shadow, a low warm dawn reaching them
scenes.emotional_tone: light crossing darkness to find a people
scenes.subject_block: a distant people of a far land standing at the edge of deep shadow, a warm dawn light reaching across the dark ground toward them from a single source low on the horizon, their faces just catching the first light, the land beyond still in night, the light crossing the darkness to find them
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of light reaching a people in darkness
macro_elements: the warm dawn on the horizon

scenes.title: The Empty Tomb
scenes.purpose: the One who died and rose — the gospel's engine
scenes.rationale: the empty tomb grounds 'died and rose' without a figure
scenes.visible_elements: an open rock tomb, stone rolled aside, dawn light, folded grave-linen
scenes.subject_block: an open rock-hewn tomb cut into a hillside, the great round stone rolled fully aside from the dark doorway, pale dawn light spilling across the empty threshold onto folded grave-linen within, the garden in cool shadow beyond, no figure present
macro_elements: the dawn light on the threshold

scenes.title: Nation After Nation
scenes.rationale: a pilgrim procession rather than another bowing crowd
scenes.visible_elements: a long procession of pilgrims of many lands on a road toward distant light
scenes.subject_block: a long procession of pilgrims of many lands winding along a wide road toward a distant warm light on the horizon, figures in the dress of far peoples strung out across the landscape, the road climbing toward the light, the line of travellers stretching back beyond sight
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of peoples turning, age after age

scenes.title: Wherever You Are
scenes.purpose: the landing's turn to the single listener
scenes.rationale: one small figure turning, the global made personal
scenes.visible_elements: a lone small figure on a far road turned toward distant light
scenes.emotional_tone: one person, here and now, turning
scenes.subject_block: a single small figure alone on a far empty road at dusk, turned toward a distant warm light on the horizon, the surrounding land wide and quiet, the lone traveller small against the great expanse, the warm light ahead the only brightness
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of one person, here and now, turning

scenes.title: Room to Turn
scenes.purpose: the open way left for whoever will come
scenes.rationale: an open road to the cross rather than a literal altar call
scenes.visible_elements: a worn open road leading to a cross on a low rise at dawn
scenes.emotional_tone: an open way left open and lit
scenes.subject_block: a worn open road leading toward a cross on a low rise at dawn, warm gold light widening behind it, the path clear and unobstructed, no figure upon it yet, the way left open and lit toward the cross
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of an open way left for whoever will come
macro_elements: the open road toward the cross
the cross on the low rise

scenes.title: The Whole Earth at Dawn
scenes.purpose: close on the cross over the whole earth at dawn
scenes.rationale: the song that swept the earth, lands bright beneath the cross
scenes.visible_elements: the cross cresting a vast world horizon at dawn, light sweeping far lands
scenes.emotional_tone: a song that swept the whole earth
scenes.subject_block: the cross cresting a vast world horizon at dawn, warm gold light sweeping outward across far lands, distant seas and remote mountains catching the first light under a widening sky, the single cross high at the centre and the whole earth opening bright beneath it
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of a song that swept the whole earth
macro_elements: the cross at the horizon's crest
the gold light sweeping the lands

plan.rationale: 14 scenes carrying the global reach of the cross honestly. Ten singles anchor the literal beats (lone cross, scroll, hero cross, small Golgotha, light to the gentiles, empty tomb, pilgrim procession, lone turner, open road, cross over the world) and four unified scenes carry the breadth and echoes (all the ends, the gospel goes out, the Abraham promise, the kindreds worshipping). Two nt-gospel-link scenes (hero cross, gospel goes out) and two NT-anchored ot-echo scenes (Gen 22:18->Gal 3:8 Abraham; Isaiah 49:6->Acts 13:47 light to the Gentiles) satisfy the mix. Rejected: a vertical heaven-opens light-beam (cliche), a modern globe/map (anachronism), and stacking five identical bowing-crowd scenes (varied to gathered worship / procession / first-light people / lone turner instead).

panel.note: The arc turns from one to all: hook [1] 'One Man, Alone' -> proof [4] 'The Reach of the Cross' -> conviction [8] 'Kindreds of the Nations' -> landing [12]/[14] 'Wherever You Are'/'The Whole Earth at Dawn'. Drives the global turn, not just illustrates.

Guards held: [7] 'In Thee All Nations Blessed' (Gen 22:18 -> Gal 3:8) and [9] 'A Light to the Gentiles' (Isa 49:6 -> Acts 13:47) are real NT-anchored echoes; the global reach is the psalm's own v27 plus historical spread, not a forced verse-citation. No modern globe/map.
[8] kindreds + [11] procession both risk a generic 'nations' look; differentiated (gathered worship vs road procession) but render with real specificity of dress/land so they don't read as stock.
jesus_variant 'passion' on the crucified scenes [1][3][4]; distant/bare crosses [5][13][14] correctly null; no drift.
Subject_blocks state-only and banned-token clean (no 'frame'); 14 scenes within the rich window; each earns its slot.
'Light to the nations' could invite the heaven-beam cliche, but [9] uses a low-horizon dawn across dark ground, not a vertical god-beam — an honest, non-predictable payoff.

gates.evidence: Ps 22:27 scroll, the cross, empty tomb, Abraham/stars, light-to-gentiles all defensible; no invented narrative; no unwarranted halos.
Every beat id in ['hook', 'point', 'proof', 'conviction', 'landing'] has >=1 supporting scene.
10 single + 4 unified; anchors vary (lone cross, scroll, world horizon, road, ship, stars, peoples, tomb).
Global reach honest (psalm's own prophecy + history + NT-anchored echoes); no overclaim, no universalism.
All subject_block + mood_block content clear of the banned-token list.

panel.note: Independent read: the one->all turn is carried structurally (lone cross open, cross-reach proof, kindreds conviction, whole-earth close); not a slideshow.

The two ot-echoes are genuinely NT-anchored (Gal 3:8 quotes Gen 22:18; Acts 13:47 quotes Isa 49:6); 'turn unto the LORD' applied to the crucified-risen Christ is sound. No overclaim of v27 as a cited NT fulfilment.
The several 'far lands / distant peoples' scenes must be rendered with real topographic and costume specificity or they blur together; the lone-cross open and empty-tomb keep it grounded.
Crucified scenes passion-consistent; bare/distant crosses null; empty tomb null — coherent.
State-only, banned-token clean, 14 scenes; framings well distributed (5 wide, 6 mid, 2 close, 1 low-angle).
Predictable risk on 'nations worshipping', mitigated by the Abraham-under-stars echo and the single lone turner at the landing.

gates.evidence: All literal scenes defensible; no invented detail; no unwarranted halos.
Every beat id in ['hook', 'point', 'proof', 'conviction', 'landing'] has >=1 supporting scene.
Honest global reach; NT-anchored echoes; no universalism.
All subject_block + mood_block content clear of the banned-token list.
